findings.title: IRS domestic migration extract appears to use foreign-migration code 97 instead of US-total code 96
findings.description: The review claims that `build_county_outcome_panel.py` functions `load_native_inflow()` and `load_native_outflow()` filter IRS SOI migration rows with `y1_statefips == "97"` and `y2_statefips == "97"`. In standard IRS SOI migration dictionaries, `96` denotes total US migration and `97` denotes total foreign migration. If true, the script is extracting foreign inflows/outflows, renaming them as `us_inflow_returns_2022_23` / `us_outflow_returns_2022_23`, and using them to compute `net_us_migration_share_2022_23`, which invalidates the native mobility / native sorting interpretation.
findings.fix: Change the IRS filter from state FIPS `97` to `96` after verifying the exact dictionary for the 2022-2023 release, then rebuild the county panel and downstream migration analyses.

findings.title: Pretrend falsification baseline is endogenous because permit capacity includes 2019 while testing 2018-2019 outcomes
findings.description: The review argues that the falsification script evaluates a `2018-2019` QCEW pretrend against a permit baseline from `2017-2019` via `extract_pre_surge_permits()`. Because 2019 permits can be downstream of 2018-2019 local growth, the baseline is not strictly antecedent and may introduce reverse causality into the pretrend test.
findings.fix: Restrict the pre-surge capacity baseline to years strictly before the outcome window, e.g. aggregate permits only through 2017 for the `2018-2019` pretrend specification.

findings.title: Annual-average QCEW windows spanning 2020 blur the pandemic shock and may destabilize threshold searches
findings.description: The review states that `build_county_outcome_panel.py` computes `2019-2020` and `2020-2021` log changes using QCEW rows filtered with `qtr == "A"` (annual averages). Annual averages for 2020 mix pre-lockdown and lockdown quarters, creating substantial measurement noise. The reviewer argues this likely contributes to the reported diffuse and unstable threshold surface.
findings.fix: Avoid annual log-change windows that touch 2020, or switch to quarterly QCEW data (e.g. Q4-to-Q4 comparisons) for pandemic and post-pandemic analyses.

findings.title: County panel does not explicitly handle QCEW privacy suppressions, risking sample-composition bias
findings.description: The review notes that `build_county_outcome_panel.py` drops duplicate or missing FIPS but does not explicitly model BLS privacy suppressions for small counties. If suppressed counties become `pd.NA` and fall out of log-change calculations, the resulting panel can systematically overweight urban and suburban counties and distort estimated capacity-frontier dynamics.
findings.fix: Audit suppressed or missing QCEW county observations, quantify attrition by county type, and add explicit handling such as flags, imputation rules, or robustness checks for sample-selection bias.

findings.title: IRS migration is treated as a generic native-mobility proxy despite undercoverage of low-income movers
findings.description: The review argues that IRS migration data capture tax filers, yet the memo uses the series as a broad proxy for native mobility. This may structurally undercount low-income natives and recent movers who do not file promptly or at all, especially the population most relevant to low-skill labor-market displacement claims.
findings.fix: Qualify the construct as tax-filer migration rather than generic native mobility, and validate or supplement it with additional mobility sources that better cover low-income movers.

findings.title: Hardcoded occupants-per-unit scalar may systematically mis-scale housing capacity
findings.description: The review flags a hardcoded `OCCUPANTS_PER_UNIT = 2.5` in the falsification workflow. The reviewer argues this embeds a single household-size assumption that may be inappropriate where immigrant household formation is larger, causing the permit-derived capacity ratio to be systematically mismeasured.
findings.fix: Parameterize the occupants-per-unit assumption or replace it with county-level external household-size estimates such as ACS-based values by FIPS.

findings.title: Single-threshold search may be the wrong model for a diffuse cutoff surface
findings.description: The review interprets the reported diffuse threshold surface as evidence against a stable scalar cutoff and in favor of heterogeneous treatment effects. Continuing to search for one universal threshold may be a misspecified modeling strategy, especially if effects vary with state housing elasticity or similar context variables.
findings.fix: Replace or complement the scalar threshold search with a heterogeneity-aware method such as a causal forest, margin tree, or threshold model conditioned on state-level elasticity measures.

findings.title: Native-mobility threshold and null analyses should be rerun after correcting the IRS migration coding
findings.description: Because the review claims the migration outcome was built from foreign IRS flows rather than domestic flows, it concludes that prior `threshold_null` and `threshold_surface` results for native sorting are not interpretable. Downstream falsification summaries based on the mislabeled migration outcome therefore need to be regenerated.
findings.fix: After correcting the IRS FIPS mapping, rerun the migration-related threshold/null analyses and refresh summary outputs such as `county_capacity_falsification_summary.json`.

findings.title: Annual causal claims for COVID-overlap years should be explicitly blocked until quarterly QCEW is used
findings.description: The review recommends suspending or demoting annual causal claims for windows overlapping 2020 because annual averages confound pre-pandemic and lockdown periods. It specifically asks for memos to acknowledge that the wage/employment split cannot be finalized without quarterly QCEW bridging late 2019 to late 2021.
findings.fix: Update analytical writeups to mark annual 2020-overlap results as provisional artifacts of data aggregation, and require quarterly QCEW before making final causal claims for that period.
